states.question: What is the time complexity of the 0/1 Knapsack bottom-up algorithm with N items and Capacity W?
states.explanation: Filling the DP table takes O(N * W) time. Since W is not bound by a polynomial of input size, this is pseudo-polynomial.

states.analogy_description: You are a burglar with a backpack of capacity W. You look at items. For each item, you decide: steal it (consume capacity, add value) or leave it. You optimize value without breaking the backpack.

whiteboard_data.title: Knapsack Matrix Lookups
whiteboard_data.description: Checking max value by choosing inclusion vs exclusion.

steps.details: Steal item i? Compare dp[i-1][w] vs value[i] + dp[i-1][w-weight[i]]

states.exercise_description: Write a recursive 0/1 Knapsack function `knapsack_rec(weights, values, W, n)` that returns the maximum value without cache.
Write a bottom-up tabulated 0/1 Knapsack function `knapsack_tab(weights, values, W)` that returns the max value.

states.prompt: Explain Introduction to Dynamic Programming II in your own words to a peer. Use an analogy from daily life.

flashcards.back: You cannot take fractional portions of an item; you must either take it fully (1) or leave it (0).
Because its optimization decision problem is NP-complete.
